objective: Complete the missing step between lesson and behavior.
question: What qualifies a lesson to permanently change organism behavior?
core_rule: A lesson may permanently change organism behavior only after repeated receipt-verified observations support it, no unresolved contradiction defeats it, no operator override blocks it, and the resulting behavior rule is written as a durable artifact that future execution can cite.
permanent_means: Default organism behavior until explicitly superseded, expired, or operator-revoked. Permanent does not mean irreversible or impossible to update.

chain.definition: A receipt-backed event, failure, blocker, or successful action that can be read and hashed.
cannot_advance_if: status is invented from chat or memory only

chain.definition: A proposed lesson extracted from one or more observations. It is not yet a behavior rule.
cannot_advance_if: lesson is too broad for the source evidence

chain.definition: The gate that decides whether a candidate lesson is strong enough to become behavior.

repetition_threshold.matching_rule: Observations match when they share the same failure_signature_hash or an operator-approved equivalence key.
repetition_threshold.dedupe_rule: Two identical failures produce one behavior rule with evidence_count = 2, not two rules.

contradiction_check.pass_condition: No unresolved contradictory receipt or rule defeats the candidate.

receipt_verification.pass_condition: Each supporting observation has a readable receipt path and stable hash, and each hash is recorded in the validation record.

chain.definition: The act of writing the validated lesson into a durable behavior rule artifact.
cannot_advance_if: behavior rule has no trigger or action

chain.definition: A durable rule that future packets and executions must consult.
chain.supersession_condition: May be superseded by operator override, stronger contradictory receipts, failed future execution, or a narrower replacement rule.

chain.definition: A later packet or action behaves differently because it cites and follows the behavior rule.

qualification_rule.name: lesson_to_permanent_behavior_rule
if: all supporting observations have matching failure_signature_hash or approved equivalence key
all supporting observations have receipt verification PASS
human_gate.status == APPROVED when action is irreversible or high consequence
then: set behavior_rule.evidence_count to number of matching verified observations
write behavior_rule to durable file-backed surface
future packets in scope must cite behavior_rule_id
future execution must produce proof_artifact_path before BEHAVIOR_CHANGE_PROVEN

result.future_execution_requirement: Next in-scope packet must cite behavior_rule_id and produce proof artifact showing changed behavior.

no_go: Do not assimilate a single failure into permanent behavior.
Do not assimilate without receipt hashes.
Do not assimilate when contradiction is unresolved.
Do not assimilate against operator override.
Do not assimilate irreversible or high-consequence behavior without Human Gate approval.
Do not claim future behavior changed until a later packet/action cites the behavior_rule_id and produces a proof artifact.
natural_death_condition: This rule evaluation ends when it writes one behavior rule, writes a blocked validation record, or writes a rejected candidate record. It does not remain alive as a watcher or daemon.
pass_condition: Two identical receipt-verified failures produce one durable behavior change.
